--- Page 2 ---
Moreover, we now know that certain
ultra-processed foods, stress, a sedentary
lifestyle, and tobacco damage the microbiota and
worsen the disease.
AND SO, TREATING YOUR
MICROBIOTA, IS IT TREATING
YOUR IBD?
So, there are currently several avenues
for the future:
Probiotics and prebiotics: Can we
use bacteria (probiotics) to calm
inflammation? The scientific world has
not reached a consensus to date on the
benefit of taking a probiotic or prebiotic;
studies are ongoing.
Fecal transplantation: this involves
transferring the microbiota from a
healthy person to a patient with IBD.
The results are interesting, however
they have not yet allowed a conclusion
about a benefit for IBD.
Treatments targeted at the microbiota:
New drugs are being tested to change
intestinal bacteria and reduce
inflammation.
Personalized nutrition: Some
scientists are trying to adapt diets
according to each person’s microbiota
to help calm symptoms.
HOW CAN I ACT ON
MY MICROBIOTA?
